name: Nongying Li
headline: Quantitative Data Science master's student focused on LLM/agent workflows, NLP, machine learning evaluation, causal inference, and psychometrics.

experiences.title: Research Assistant, Method Center, University of Tübingen
bullets: Supported research on time-dependent confounding in causal inference.
Explored RNN-based counterfactual outcome prediction workflows.
Contributed to experiment design, data preprocessing, model implementation, and reproducible analysis pipelines.

projects.title: Agentic AI Research: CALE Framework for LLM Reasoning and Evaluation
bullets: Designed and evaluated an agentic AI workflow for structured reasoning, response generation, and automated evaluation.
Built Python-based pipelines for prompt construction, model inference, result parsing, and comparative analysis.
Summarized LLM behavior into reproducible reports and visual materials.

projects.title: NLP Project: Joint Entity and Relation Extraction
bullets: Implemented and compared CasRel, TDEER, TPLinker, and UniRel for extracting structured triples from unstructured text.
Evaluated model suitability for overlapping entities and complex relation patterns.
Created case-study comparisons to explain trade-offs between model architectures.

projects.title: Mask-Aware VAE Imputation for Large-Scale Assessments
bullets: Developed mask-aware VAE workflows for missing-value imputation in large response matrices.
Compared results with 2PL Item Response Theory baselines.
Evaluated missing-cell prediction accuracy and market-basket reporting stability.

projects.title: DenseNet for Galaxy Morphology Classification
bullets: Preprocessed image data for clarity, resolution, and noise quality.
Implemented DenseNet and tuned learning rate and batch size.
Used Grad-CAM and smooth saliency maps to interpret model predictions.

notes: Public GitHub profile intentionally omits phone number, date of birth, student ID, transcript details, and private documents.
